calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Victor Waid for administrators, Allen Emmons and Kathleen Emmons.
Kathleen Emmons also present via zoom.

The court stated that it struggles with the fee requests for extraordinary services because there is no provision in the probate codes which allows this. Mr. Waid stated that he does not have a statute ready to offer, that he had these granted in other courts from his 50 years of experience, that he has laid out all the facts on his verified filings, that his clients share the same position, that he has no interest at this stage in his life and practice to take anymore from this estate, that he cannot argue against his own investment of time, that his clients are entitled to extraordinary fees for the sale and for a very complicated case, that he had assistance from 2 very good paralegals, that it would be hole in his pocket if extraordinary fees are not granted, and requested the court to take the matter under submission. The court stated its appreciation of Mr. Waid being upfront and takes the second and final account and report of co-administrators, etc. under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Probate Accounting -Part 1 Of 4
Off Calendar Jan-16-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Victor Waid appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court stated that it needs the Seller's closing statement. Mr. Waid stated that the escrow statement is included in the August 19th filing (page 65 of the original amended petition), that it's from Fidelity and that the numbers have not changed. The Court stated that the pages of the document are not numbered, and that an escrow statement and a closing statement are not the same thing. Mr. Waid stated that the Richmond property was a stock sale, that they do not issue closing statements for that, that the estate got full credit in that amount of $1,412.86 for the HOA dues and requested a six month continuance to do distribution stating that he received two IRS requests. The Second Amended First Account and Report of Personal Representative, etc. is under submission. Mr. Waid is to file a declaration addressing the HOA dues with the closing statement attached and provide courtesy copies to the Court by the end of the week. If the materials are not received by Friday, the Court will deny the petition. Status on close of estate is set for July 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Trausch. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-06-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Victor Waid appeared by telephone for Allen Emmons and Kathleen Emmons (Administrators).

The Court stated that it needs a copy of the petition and a proposed order on the status report. Mr. Waid stated that he received a list from the Probate Examiner and that he completed the list of items, but there is a hold up with the IRS. Mr. Waid also stated that they need clearance from the IRS for all of the taxes paid prior to the Decedent's death and after, suggested taking this accounting petition off calendar, stated that he will do another accounting for 2017 and then do a final, and that he had not filed a status report. The Court Clerk reminded Counsel that a status report was filed in August of 2018. The First Account, etc. is off calendar. The Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration is continued to February 20, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to provide a copy of the petition and a proposed order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Feb-22-2017 Continued To Jun-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Appearing through Court call Victor Waid for Allen Emmons (petitioner).

Mr. Waid reported that the missing page from his inventory and appraisal was the legal description of the Gough Street Property, that he will file the corrected inventory and appraisal, that both properties were sold for more than their appraised amounts and that he will get a reappraisal. The Court asked the amount of cash he has on hand. Mr. Waid stated that he has $800,000.00 in cash and that he will end up with 4 accounts when he breaks up the cash into units. Furthermore Mr. Waid spoke about the property bankruptcy proceedings and requested a 6 month continuance. The Court stated that a partial distribution can be made. Mr. Waid argued that would double the work, that there is only 1 heir and she is in her 90's and living in a board and care home. Court investigator Sandra Hilton stated that a property that has been sold does not need to be reappraised.

The Court granted the petition for approval of report on status of administration (subject to receipt and review of the order) and continued this matter to June 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera and Nancy Wong. Court Investigator: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)
